evals.prompt: Execute /build on this project. The plan is at .planning/phases/01-auth/01-01-PLAN.md. Walk through exactly what you would do step by step — which agents you'd dispatch, what prompts they'd get, what you'd check between waves. This is a dry run — describe each action, don't actually execute.
evals.expected_output: Should reference implementer-prompt.md template, dispatch general-purpose subagents per task in waves, run spec gate with code-reviewer agent between waves, dispatch gsd-integration-checker in background (or explain why skipped), use code-reviewer for quality review at end, mention auto-mode check

assertions.text: References implementer-prompt.md template for subagent prompts
Dispatches per-wave spec gate using code-reviewer agent type
Uses code-reviewer agent type for final quality review (not generic)
Mentions auto-mode check (AUTO_MODE or workflow.auto_advance)
Correctly identifies 2 waves (tasks 1-2 parallel, tasks 3-4 parallel)
Mentions WAVE_START_SHA tracking for spec gate diffs
References deferred items logging to phase directory

evals.prompt: I'm getting 3 independent test failures: auth tests fail with 'session not found' in src/lib/__tests__/auth.test.ts, billing tests fail with 'invoice amount NaN' in src/lib/__tests__/billing.test.ts, and notification tests fail with 'channel undefined' in src/lib/__tests__/notifications.test.ts. These are in different subsystems. Use /fix to handle this. Dry run — describe what you'd do step by step.
evals.expected_output: Should triage as PARALLEL (3+ independent failures), dispatch gsd-debugger agents (not generic), mention deferred items tracking, then TDD fix per subsystem

assertions.text: Triages depth as PARALLEL (3+ independent failures in different subsystems)
Dispatches gsd-debugger specialized agents (not generic general-purpose)
Mentions deferred items tracking to deferred-items.md
Plans TDD fix (RED-GREEN-REFACTOR) after root cause identified
Plans verification via skills/verification-before-completion

evals.prompt: I need to refactor: extract the shared validation logic from 3 API route handlers (src/app/api/auth/signup/route.ts, src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts, src/app/api/boards/create/route.ts) into a reusable validation middleware at src/lib/validation.ts. Use /refactor. Dry run — walk through what you'd do.
evals.expected_output: Should scope blast radius, capture baseline tests, plan atomic steps, use code-reviewer agent for review, follow iron law (revert-on-red)

assertions.text: Scopes blast radius (identifies 3+ files, maps dependencies)
Captures baseline tests before starting
Plans atomic steps (one structural change at a time)
Uses code-reviewer specialized agent for review step
Mentions iron law: revert immediately if tests go red
Plans verification-before-completion at end

evals.prompt: I want to plan the next feature: real-time task status updates using Supabase Realtime so team members see changes instantly without refreshing. Use /plan. Dry run — describe what you'd do step by step, including which agents you'd dispatch.
evals.expected_output: Should check phase matching against ROADMAP, detect research need (Supabase Realtime), invoke brainstorming skill, discuss implementation gray areas, derive must_haves, create PLAN.md with wave structure, run plan-check

assertions.text: Checks phase matching against ROADMAP.md (this doesn't match existing phases)
Detects need for research (Supabase Realtime is external/unfamiliar API)
Invokes brainstorming skill for design exploration
Plans to derive must_haves (truths, artifacts, key_links)
Plans plan-check validation before presenting to user
Mentions discuss implementation step to lock decisions in CONTEXT.md

evals.prompt: Read .claude/skills/auto/auto-orchestrator.cjs and explain how the auto-orchestrator handles SPEC.md files during build sessions. Specifically: (1) how does it discover a SPEC.md alongside a PLAN.md, (2) what happens if a PLAN.md frontmatter references a spec via the 'spec:' field but the spec file is missing, (3) how is spec awareness passed to the build session? Dry run — describe the code flow, don't execute.
evals.expected_output: Should describe SPEC.md discovery via sibling path derivation (XX-NN-PLAN.md → XX-NN-SPEC.md), missing spec warning via log(), and specHint appended to build session prompt

assertions.text: Describes spec path derivation from plan path (replacing -PLAN.md with -SPEC.md)
Mentions warning/log when spec: frontmatter field references a missing file
Describes specHint being appended to the build session prompt
Notes this happens in the executeStep function's build case

evals.prompt: Read .claude/skills/auto/auto-orchestrator.cjs and explain how the auto-orchestrator creates a cross-phase reflection feedback loop. Specifically: (1) how are reflection findings extracted from completed phase SUMMARYs, (2) how are they passed to subsequent planning sessions in both the 3-wave pipeline and sequential fallback modes, (3) what happens when there is no ## Reflection section in a SUMMARY? Dry run — describe the code flow.
evals.expected_output: Should describe extractReflectionFindings helper, phase_states-based completed phase detection in 3-wave mode, directory scan in sequential mode, and graceful handling of missing Reflection sections

assertions.text: Describes extractReflectionFindings function that reads ## Reflection sections from SUMMARY.md files
Explains 3-wave pipeline uses phase_states to find completed ('built') phases for reflection
Explains sequential fallback scans phase directories for plans+summaries to find completed phases
Notes graceful handling when ## Reflection section is missing (returns empty, does not error)

evals.prompt: Read .claude/skills/auto/auto-orchestrator.cjs and explain how --resume detects and reports task-level state files for partial build recovery. Also explain how the build session is informed about existing task state. Describe: (1) where task state files are scanned, (2) the file path convention used, (3) how completed vs remaining tasks are counted, (4) how build sessions receive task state awareness. Dry run — describe the code flow.
evals.expected_output: Should describe .planning/build/task-*-state.md scanning during --resume, status regex matching for completed/pending counts, log message about partial build detection, and taskStateHint appended to build prompt

assertions.text: Describes scanning .planning/build/ for task-*-state.md files during --resume
Mentions regex matching for status: completed/done vs in-progress/pending in state file content
Describes the partial build detection log message with completed/remaining counts
Explains taskStateHint appended to build session prompt for resume awareness
